title: storage-v3 #7: test sweep to v3 assertions
body:
# Goal

Port the 421-test workspace from v2-shape assertions to
v3-shape assertions. Audit every test that pokes at the
`issues` bookmark or asserts on commit-on-bookmark behavior,
update it to assert on per-issue-ref shape instead.

End state: `cargo nextest run --workspace` returns 421+ tests
passing on a v3-only codebase (after this ticket lands, no
test still asserts the v2 dance is in use).

# Why now

Ticket 7 of 8 in the v3 storage epic (`bd98097`). Depends on
tickets 1–6. After this ticket lands, the v3 implementation is
end-to-end green; ticket 8 (guard removal + CLAUDE.md rewrite)
can ship without test-suite risk.

# Approach

The 421 tests split roughly into:

**Tier A: CLI-surface tests.** Drive `jjf` verbs and assert on
the `--json` output or stdout/stderr. These should stay green
WITHOUT change — the CLI surface doesn't move in v3. If any
break, that's a sign tickets 1–6 leaked behavior changes into
the surface. Investigate and fix the underlying code, not the
test.

**Tier B: Storage-internal tests.** Reach into `Storage::*`
methods directly and assert on internal shape — bookmark name,
file paths, commit count, trailer shape. Audit each:

- If it asserts `bookmark exists` or `jj file show
  -r bookmarks(issues)` or `issues/<id>.json` paths → port
  to assert `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` exists and has the right
  blob.
- If it asserts on trailer-block shape → leave alone (the
  trailer format doesn't change in v3).
- If it asserts on op-replay correctness → port the chain
  walk to the per-issue ref.

**Tier C: Migration tests.** Specifically
`v1_to_v2_migration_preserves_history`. Add a sibling test
`v2_to_v3_migration_preserves_history` modeled on it.

**Tier D: HEAD-drift tests.** Any test that asserted "after a
mutation, jj working copy is at root() or descendant" → invert
the assertion: "after a mutation, jj working copy is unchanged
and git HEAD is unchanged." This is the safety net for the
core property v3 delivers.

# Scope

In scope:
- Grep the test tree for `bookmarks(issues)`, `issues/`,
  `try_commit_dance`, `bugs bookmark`, and similar substrings
  → audit each hit.
- Port Tier B and Tier C tests.
- Add the v2 → v3 migration test.
- Add a regression test for HEAD-no-drift on every v3
  mutation kind (Tier D).
- Remove tests that assert on v2 behavior we explicitly want
  gone (e.g. tests that assert the 4-CLI dance runs the
  expected jj subcommands).

Out of scope:
- New test infrastructure. Reuse the existing
  `IntegrationFixture` / temp-repo helpers.
- Performance tests beyond the existing
  `cache_hit_avoids_rebuild_n_issues`.

# Acceptance

- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green on v3-only code
      (no fallback to v2 dance for fresh repos).
- [ ] No test still asserts the v2 dance is invoked.
- [ ] At least one test per v3 mutation kind asserts
      "HEAD does not drift, jj working copy is clean."
- [ ] A v2 → v3 migration integration test exists and passes.
- [ ] Test count is >= 421 (we add a few, port the rest).

# Pointers

- v1 → v2 reference test:
  `v1_to_v2_migration_preserves_history`.
- Cache test: `cache_hit_avoids_rebuild_n_issues`.
- Trailer test: `trailer_block_shape_walker_finds_no_orphans_on_real_bookmark`
  — this one likely needs a v3 sibling.
- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`.

# Out-of-scope reminder

Do NOT update tests by silencing them
(e.g. `#[ignore]`). Either port them or delete with a one-line
explanation. The test suite is the spec of behavior; weakening
it weakens the contract.
